name: Des Moines (United States)

colors.symbolism: the field, recalling the rivers that converge at Des Moines
the wave-form bands representing the Des Moines and Raccoon rivers
the hoist triangle, representing Fort Des Moines and the city's military origins

description: A blue field crossed by three white wave-form bands rising from hoist to fly, with a red triangle filling the hoist edge.
history: Adopted by the Des Moines City Council in 2018, replacing an earlier flag. The design was selected from a public competition organised in cooperation with the local arts community.

features.role: three white wave bands across the blue field
features.symbolism: The three wave-form bands represent the Des Moines River and Raccoon River that converge in the city, and the bridges that span them.
Recalls Fort Des Moines, the U.S. Army post around which the city was founded in 1843.
